# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
|---------|:---------:|
| 0.2.x   | ✅        |
| < 0.2   | ❌        |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Please do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.**

Report security issues by emailing the maintainer directly or by using
[GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability).

You should receive a response within **72 hours**. If the issue is confirmed,
a patch will be released as soon as possible depending on severity.

## Scope

In scope:
- Vulnerabilities in ArtiShield's Rust source code
- Dependency vulnerabilities that affect ArtiShield
- Docker image security issues
- Incorrect threat-detection logic that could mislead operators

Out of scope:
- Vulnerabilities in arti itself (report to the Tor Project)
- Social engineering attacks
